description: Memory architecture: Three-Layer Model + save triggers + read patterns + compaction handling + grep contract

working_memory.json.role: current-session operational memory — focus_node, open_pages, next_steps_todo, dead_ends, query_summary
working_memory.json.lifespan: short: one session. Cleared on dismissal/compaction.

hot.md.role: long-term snapshot — active project/session context, wiki changes summary. Survives compaction.
hot.md.lifespan: between sessions. Updated after important actions AND on any wiki page change.
hot.md.rw: agent writes snapshot (not full log tape)

structure.active_project: WORK_MODE: project — name, focus_node, related_pages, findings, next_steps
structure.system_state: Active Threads + Recent Changes (updated on every wiki create/update)

log.md.role: append-only chronicle of all actions and wiki changes. Chronological tape.

session_start.action: restore-hot-cache.sh → read hot.md for project/session context
grep -m 20 '' wiki/log.md | tail -20 → understand previous discussion

save_triggers.action: write query intent + project_context to hot.md Active Session Context
write action_summary + wiki_pages_used + key_findings to hot.md
update System State → Recent Changes section

step_2_action_done.hot_section: Active Session Context → pages read, key findings

steps.action: restore-hot-cache.sh → read hot.md for active project/session context

log_read_pattern.when: session_start if working_memory.json empty or stale (>30 days without updates)
log_read_pattern.goal: understand previous discussion context, active projects, where we stopped
log_read_pattern.constraint: NEVER read entire log (~250+ lines expensive in tokens)

constraints.value: Write hot.md immediately after action, NOT at session end
hot.md = summary slice only, never full log copy
After context compaction: call restore-hot-cache.sh || true
System State section updated on every wiki create/update
Never append to JSON files — read full file → modify → write complete document
wiki/log.md is append-only. NEVER cat > or write(). Always use cat >> (append).

context_bubble.description: No more than 3 active pages in context simultaneously

natural_memory_translation.description: Translate timestamps to human-time: 'the day before yesterday', 'a week ago'
natural_memory_translation.rule: Use 'we' instead of 'system/agent' when speaking about shared experience

golden_rules.value: Write hot.md immediately, not at session end
hot.md = summary slice, never full log copy
System State section updated on every wiki create/update
